ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR PLAINTIFF JAYDEN BELL'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF BING CHEN AND TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF HIS RECORDED STATEMENT AND SEEK SANCTION HEARING SET FOR JUN-05-2017 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JAYDEN BELL'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF BING CHEN AND TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF HIS RECORDED STATEMENT AND SEEK SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART AND THE REQUESTS FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS ON BOTH SIDES IS DENIED. THE DEPOSITION OF MS. CHEN MUST OCCUR ON A MUTUALLY CONVENIENT DATE WITHIN 30 DAYS. MS. CHEN IS NOT REQUIRED TO PRODUCE THE RECORDED STATEMENT. THAT STATEMENT IS COVERED BY THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE PER SOLTANI-RASTEGAR V. SUPERIOR COURT (1989) 208 CAL. APP. 3D 424. MS. CHEN DID NOT WAIVE THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE AS TO THE STATEMENT AND ADEQUATELY OBJECTED TO THE PRODUCTION OF THE STATEMENT ON THE GROUNDS OF THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE WHEN SHE ASSERTED THE OBJECTION ON THE GROUNDS THAT THE STATEMENT WAS A "PRIVILEGED RECORDED STATEMENT." NEITHER SIDE LACKED SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION FOR THE POSITIONS IT TOOK ON THIS MOTION. PARTIES CAN COME TO THE EX-PARTE CALENDAR AND PRODUCE THE VIDEO TAPE, IF THERE IS A DISPUTE ENCOUNTERED BY THE PARTIES. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF JAYDEN BELL'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER LIMITING HIS DEPOSITION TO TWO HOURS IS GRANTED IN SUBSTANTIAL PART AND HIS REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. JAYDEN'S DEPOSITION MUST OCCUR ON A MUTUALLY AGREEABLE DATE WITHIN 30 DAYS, MAY BE VIDEOTAPED, AND MAY NOT EXCEED TWO HOURS IN LENGTH, EXCLUDING BREAKS AND ANY STATEMENTS MADE BY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. IF, AFTER THE CONCLUSION OF THE DEPOSITION, DEFENDANTS HAVE ADDITIONAL DEPOSITION QUESTIONS FOR JAYDEN, AT THEIR OPTION, THEY MAY PROPOUND THOSE QUESTIONS AS WRITTEN DEPOSITION QUESTIONS PER CCP 2028.010 AND/OR THEY MAY REQUEST ADDITIONAL TIME FOR THE ORAL DEPOSITION OF JAYDEN. IF DEFENDANTS REQUEST ADDITIONAL TIME FOR THE ORAL DEPOSITION OF JAYDEN, THE PARTIES MUST MEET AND CONFER IN A GOOD FAITH EFFORT TO ACCOMMODATE THE MEDICAL NEEDS OF JADEN AND THE DISCOVERY NEEDS OF DEFENDANTS. IF THE PARTIES CANNOT REACH AN AGREEMENT, DEFENDANTS MAY MAKE AN EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ADDITIONAL ORAL DEPOSITION TIME AND IN THEIR APPLICATION MUST IDENTIFY THE AREAS OF INQUIRY THEY WISH TO COVER AND PROVIDE A VIDEOTAPE OR TRANSCRIPT OF THE PREVIOUS SESSION OF THE DEPOSITION. BECAUSE DEFENDANTS' POSITIONS DO NOT LACK SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION, NO MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

Plaintiff Jayden Bell'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Bing Chen And To Compel Production Of His Recorded Statement And Seek Sanction
Off Calendar Jun-05-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. TERESA LI OF THE LAW OFFICES OF BEN GLEN, APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SABRINA BERDUX COHEN, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 1-415-554-3929 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CCSF;
ATTY. RICHARD OZOWKSI OF THE LAW OFFICES OF MARK LOBRE, TEL. NO. 1-415-219-7119 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BING CHEN.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Jayden Bell'S Motion for a protective order limiting his deposition to two hours is GRANTED in substantial part and his request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. Jayden's deposition must occur on a mutually agreeable date within 30 days, may be videotaped, and may not exceed two hours in length, excluding breaks and any statements made by plaintiff's counsel. If, after the conclusion of the deposition, defendants have additional deposition questions for Jayden, at their option, they may propound those questions as written deposition questions per CCP 2028.010 and/or they may request additional time for the oral deposition of Jayden. If defendants request additional time for the oral deposition of Jayden, the parties must meet and confer in a good faith effort to accommodate the medical needs of Jaden and the discovery needs of defendants. If the parties cannot reach an agreement, defendants may make an ex parte application for additional oral deposition time and in their application must identify the areas of inquiry they wish to cover and provide a videotape or transcript of the previous session of the deposition. Because defendants' positions do not lack substantial justification, no monetary sanctions are awarded. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/hk

Plaintiff Jayden Bell'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Bing Chen And To Compel Production Of His Recorded Statement And Seek Sanction
Off Calendar Jun-05-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. TERESA LI OF THE LAW OFFICES OF BEN GLEN, APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SABRINA BERDUX COHEN, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 1-415-554-3929 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CCSF;
ATTY. RICHARD OZOWKSI OF THE LAW OFFICES OF MARK LOBRE, TEL. NO. 1-415-219-7119 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BING CHEN.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court modified its tentative ruling: Plaintiff Jayden Bell'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Bing Chen And To Compel Production Of His Recorded Statement And Seek Sanctions is GRANTED in part and the requests for monetary sanctions on both sides is DENIED. The deposition of Ms. Chen must occur on a mutually convenient date within 30 days. Ms. Chen is not required to produce the recorded statement. That statement is covered by the attorney-client privilege per Soltani-Rastegar v. Superior Court (1989) 208 Cal. App. 3d 424. Ms. Chen did not waive the attorney-client privilege as to the statement and adequately objected to the production of the statement on the grounds of the attorney-client privilege when she asserted the objection on the grounds that the statement was a "privileged recorded statement." Neither side lacked substantial justification for the positions it took on this motion. Parties can come to the ex-parte calendar and produce the video tape, if there is a dispute encountered by the parties. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/hk
